Railroad safety

Trebor Banstetter The Palm Beach Post May 2000
Public SafetyAgriculture & Land

In March 1999, a horrific car-train crash in West Palm Beach highlighted pressing safety concerns at railroad crossings. Using Federal Railroad Administration's database, an analysis revealed that Palm Beach County had more crashes than anywhere else in Florida, with higher injury and fatality rates.

In March 1999, a horrific car-train crash in West Palm Beach highlighted pressing safety concerns at railroad crossings. Using Federal Railroad Administration's database, an analysis revealed that Palm Beach County had more crashes than anywhere else in Florida, with higher injury and fatality rates. Findings pointed to growth in traffic and railroad activity coupled with inadequate crossing infrastructure.

The coverage provided a wider safety context, urging improvements for future traveler safety.
